editionStmt.p: First digital edition made by √âcole fran√ßaise d'Extr√™me-Orient (Paris, France), realized in collaboration with the HiSoMA Research Centre (Lyon, France) and hosted by TGIR Huma-Num (France) as Early Inscriptions of ƒÄndhrade≈õa, in 2015-2017.

This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported License. To view a copy of this license, visit http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_IdListMembers.xml file.

revisionDesc.change: EIAD file transformed to follow the DHARMA encoding structure. Metadata extracted to be checked and updated according DHARMA workflow. Done through XSLT.

div.ab:
svasti *≈õrƒ´vijayanandivarddhanavƒÅsakƒÅd devadvijagurucara·πáƒÅravinda
samƒÅrƒÅdhanƒÅdhigatavipulanirati≈õayapu·πáyanicayasya sakala
di·πÖma·πá·∏çalƒÅla·πÅkƒÅrabh≈´taya≈õasa·∏• ≈õrƒ´prabhƒÅkaravikhyƒÅtamahƒÅrƒÅja
sya s≈´nu·∏• mƒÅtƒÅpitrÃ•pƒÅdƒÅnuddhyƒÅta·∏• ≈õrutismrÃ•tivihitapadƒÅrtthƒÅvabodha
janitapraj√±ƒÅvivekaniravagƒ´tƒÅnupƒÅlitƒÅ≈õe·π£avar·πá·πáƒÅ≈õramadha
rmma·∏• prajƒÅparirak·π£a·πáopajanitapu·πáyopacayaprayojanƒÅvƒÅptarƒÅ
jyagurubhƒÅra·∏• p≈´rvvarƒÅjar·π£imaryyƒÅdƒÅnatikrama·πáƒ´yadharmma·∏• AsakrÃ•
danekani≈õitanistri·πÅ≈õasahasrasa·πÅkulƒÅtibhƒ´masa·πÅgrƒÅmabh≈´miprati
2labdhavijayƒ´ ≈õa≈õƒ´va prajƒÅnandakara·∏• udadhir iva gabhƒ´rasvatva·∏•
Aharahas trisandhyƒÅm i·π£·π≠adevatƒÅrƒÅdhanena gobh≈´mihira·πáyakanyƒÅ
pradƒÅnƒÅdibhi≈õ ca saphalƒ´krÃ•takƒÅyacittopakara·πáa·∏• paramamƒÅ
he≈õvara·∏• paramabrahma·πáya·∏• ≈õrƒ´man¬∑ prithivi≈õrƒ´m≈´larƒÅja·∏• svavi·π£ayani
vƒÅsina·∏• sarvvƒÅn evam ƒÅj√±ƒÅpayati yathƒÅ viditam astu bhavatƒÅm¬∑ bhuvanavi
dite ≈õrƒ´gu·πáapƒÅ≈õapure ≈õailendrap≈´rvvadigbhƒÅganitambe matprati·π£·π≠hƒÅpita
≈õrƒ´lokavikhyƒÅtamahƒÅvihƒÅranivƒÅsina·∏• A·π£·π≠ƒÅda≈õanikƒÅyacƒÅtu
rddi≈õƒÅryyavarabhik·π£usa·πÅghasya cƒ´varapi·πá·∏çapƒÅta≈õaiyyƒÅsanaglƒÅnapra
3pratyayabhai·π£ajyapari·π£karopabhogƒÅya tƒÅ·∏ªupƒÅkkavi·π£aye karaku·πá·∏çaka·πÅ
sƒÅryyave·∏∑ka·πá·∏çakurikima·πáimarddipallave√±canƒÅmadheyagrƒÅmamaddhyagata·∏• Apara≈õailanivƒÅsinan navakarmmika
m adhi·π£·π≠hƒÅya Ar·π≠·π≠aluvu·∏∑¬∑ nƒÅma grƒÅmas sarvvabƒÅdhaparihƒÅre·πáa mayƒÅ datta·∏• tatra na ke
na cit paribƒÅdhƒÅ kara·πáƒ´yƒÅ bhavatti cƒÅtra manugƒ´tƒÅ ≈õlokƒÅ·∏•

pravarddhamƒÅnavijayarƒÅjyasa·πÅvatsare
tracatvƒÅri·πÅ≈õati varttamƒÅne phƒÅlgu·πáamƒÅsa≈õuklapak·π£asya
trÃ•tiyƒÅyƒÅm¬∑ varttamƒÅnƒÅyƒÅ·πÅ ≈õƒÅsanam a·πÅkitam¬∑ .

app.note: The first word is engraved in the left margin.
There is no kra to correct to krÃ• . See a clear example of krÃ• in l. 10.
Krishna Sastry does not note the scribal deletion.
A kƒÅkapƒÅda is marked above the ≈õa, but no correction or insertion seems to be made nor is any expected.
According to Krishna Sastry, this must be corrected to -bƒÅdhƒÅpari-. But the reading -bƒÅdhƒÅpari- is not attested anywhere in these inscriptions.
Krishna Sastry emends to bhatrÃ•-. Neither Krishna Sastry‚Äô reading nor his emendation is correct.
Despite the reading, the interpretation of Krishna Sastry and his contemporaries must have been that this must be emended to tricatvƒÅri·πÅ≈õati, and this is indeed what must be done.

div.p: Hail! From the victorious camp of Nandivardhana, the illustrious king PrÃ•thivƒ´≈õrƒ´m≈´la, blessed by the feet of his parents; upholder of all laws on class and stage of life, in impeccable manner, through [his] intelligence and discrimination produced by [his] perception of the meanings of the words enjoined in revelation and tradition; who had attained the heavy burden of sovereignty with the purpose of accumulating merit produced by protecting his subjects; for whom the rules of the Old Royal Sage (i.e. Manu) were an unbreakable law; who had won victory more than once on battle grounds that were excessively terrifying, dense with thousands of swords sharpened many times; who, like the moon, brought pleasure to his subjects; who, like the ocean, was of profound nature; who had brought fruit to his bodily and mental activities by daily worship of his preferred deity at the three junctures and by grants of cows, lands, gold and maidens, etc.; devout worshiper of Mahe≈õvara; extremely devout; son of the celebrated great king ≈örƒ´ PrabhƒÅkara, whose fame was the ornament of all quarters of space, whose unsurpassed store of merit had been obtained by the veneration of the feet of gods, brahmins and masters ‚Äî he commands all residents of his (?) own district, as follows:

‚ÄòBe it known to you that, having endorsed (adhi·π£·π≠hƒÅya) the officer in charge of constructions and renovations residing on the Apara≈õaila (to oversee the gift), the village named Ar·π≠·π≠aluvu·∏∑, being in the middle of the villages called Karaku·πá·∏ça, Ka·πÅsƒÅryya, Ve·∏∑ka·πá·∏ça, Kuriki, Ma·πáimarddi and Pallave√±ca, in the TƒÅ·∏ªupƒÅka district, has been given by me‚Äîwith complete exemption of taxes‚Äîfor the purpose of the requisites consisting in robe, alms-food, lodging, as well as medicine to cure the sick, of the community of noble excellent monks of the four quarters and eighteen schools, residing in the great monastery called ≈örƒ´-Loka, that had been established by myself on the eastern slope of the eminent mountain at the world-renowned ≈örƒ´-Gu·πáapƒÅ≈õapura. No one may make any trouble in this connection. And there are verses pronounced by Manu on this matter:
The destiny of those who slay their master, a cow, a brahmin, a child, a woman or an ascetic, will be the one of him who seizes [the land/livelihood] that is marked by a (royal) charter.
Hear the fruit of the virtuous man who protects it scrupulously: during numerous thousands of aeons he plays in heaven with the gods.
There is not and will not be any higher gift than the gift of land. There is not and will not be any worse sin than the theft of the same.
The giver of land stays in heaven for sixty thousand years; the one who seizes [land] as well as the one who approves (of the seizing) will reside as many [years] in hell.
He who would seize land given by himself or by someone else becomes a worm in excrement and is cooked together with his ancestors.
By numerous [kings], land has been given; and by many it has been protected. Whoever holds land at a given moment, to him does the fruit then belong.
The charter has been dated in the forty-third year, current, of the victorious and prosperous reign, on the third [day], current, of the waxing fortnight of the month of PhƒÅlgu·πáa.
As long as moon, sun and oceans [last], and as long as this earth [lasts], so long may the command of ≈örƒ´ M≈´larƒÅja protect this land!
First described and edited by : 74-5, 80-2. Re-edited here from the estampages published by Krishna Sastry and after autopsy of the plates.
